title: Ownership, Borrowing, Aliasing, and API Contracts · Memory Retrieval
summary: After a delay, a release reviewer reconstructs the ownership-annotated buffer API contract from partial traces, compares two near-miss implementations, and retrieves prior bounds and lifetime rules before feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for ownership-annotated buffer API after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Classify each pointer as unique owner, shared owner, mutable borrow, immutable borrow, weak observation, or non-owning handle.
Make ownership transfer conditional on observable success and leave failure states unambiguous.
Bound every borrow by owner lifetime, mutation rules, callback duration, and asynchronous retention.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
